sanskrit:
यं सन्न्यासमिति प्राहुर्योगं तं विद्धि पाण्डव।
न ह्यसन्न्यस्तसङ्कल्पो योगी भवति कश्चन॥
transliteration: yaṁ sannyāsam iti prāhur yogaṁ taṁ viddhi pāṇḍava | na hy asannyasta-saṅkalpo yogī bhavati kaścana ||
literal_meaning: What they call renunciation, know that to be yoga, O Pāṇḍava; for without giving up selfish desire no one becomes a yogī.
contextual_meaning: True mental health is not suppression of desire but redirection—renouncing self-centered gratification and aligning actions with a larger, values-based purpose.
psychological_state: Pre-contemplative readiness to shift from ego-gratification to value-driven living; ambivalence between comfort and growth.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media for the tenth time today, feeling hollow, wondering why the likes never fill the inner gap.
anchor_text: Let go of selfish wanting; serve a purpose bigger than yourself.
